rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 21, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT KARLO, LLC'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. The Court is inclined to grant the motion to withdraw as counsel. Counsel shall bring to the hearing a completed Proposed Order on the mandatory Judicial Council form (MC-053), filling in the information at items 2 through 9. If the Court grants the order, item 5(a) will indicate that the attorney is relieved as counsel of record for client effective upon the filing of the proof of service of the signed order upon the client. No opposition filed. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 21, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF ANTWUANETTE QUEEN-POPE'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. If the Court grants the motion for withdrawal, the Court will require service on the last known address, and service according to CCP section 1011(b) and rule 3.252 of the California Rules of Court (as set forth in paragraph 6 of the mandatory Judicial Council form order (MC-053) submitted with the moving papers.) No opposition filed. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARIA BUZON et al VS. ALEXANDER ROBERT VARTAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, August 21, 2013, Line 1: Defendant ALEXANDER VARTAN'S Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery; Request for Sanctions Continued to August 29, 2013 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 21, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELEANOR BALES et al VS. UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 21, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH PLAN CALIFORNIA, UNITED HEALTHCARE SERVICES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Continued to August 28, 2013. In the Stipulation to Continue Hearing submitted to the Court on August 15, 2013, the parties requested a continuance of Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment to September 3, 2013, which is the date of trial. Law & Motion matters are not heard on the date of trial. (See SFLR 8.2A(2)(c).) If the case is settled prior to August 28, 2013 or if the parties wish to take the motion off calendar, the parties are requested to let the Court know immediately by sending an email to calendar302@sftc.org. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN BENTHAM VS. AVIGNON O'FARRELL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Doucments, Set One, And For Sanctions Against Defts And Their Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, August 21, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff RYAN BENTHAM'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One and Request for Production of Doucments, Set One, and for Sanctions Against Defendants and Their Counsel of Record Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to Interrogatories is granted as it is not opposed. Sanctions in the sum of $1040 are awarded to moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to steve@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
